name: Next.js Multi-Stage Dockerfile
description: Optimized multi-stage Docker build for Next.js with minimal final image size

steps.name: Create Dockerfile
steps.command:
cat > ${DEPLOYMENT_PATH}/Dockerfile << 'EOF'
# Stage 1: Dependencies
FROM node:${NODE_VERSION} AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json pnpm-lock.yaml ./
RUN npm install -g pnpm && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

# Stage 2: Build
FROM node:${NODE_VERSION} AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
RUN npm install -g pnpm && pnpm build

# Stage 3: Runtime
FROM node:${NODE_VERSION} AS runner
WORKDIR /app

ENV NODE_ENV production

RUN npm install -g pnpm

COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder /app/.next/static ./.next/static

EXPOSE 3000

CMD ["node", "server.js"]
EOF

steps.name: Create .dockerignore
steps.command:
cat > ${DEPLOYMENT_PATH}/.dockerignore << 'EOF'
node_modules
npm-debug.log
.git
.gitignore
README.md
.env.local
.next
dist
.turbo
EOF

steps.name: Clone repository (if not present)
steps.command: if [ ! -f ${DEPLOYMENT_PATH}/package.json ]; then cd ${DEPLOYMENT_PATH} && git clone https://github.com/${GITHUB_OWNER}/doez . || echo 'Repository may already be cloned'; fi

documentation.title: Next.js Multi-Stage Docker Build
documentation.description: Creates optimized Docker images using multi-stage builds

sections.title: Build Stages
sections.content: Stage 1 (deps): Installs dependencies. Stage 2 (builder): Builds Next.js. Stage 3 (runner): Creates minimal production image.

sections.title: Image Optimization
sections.content: Uses Alpine Linux base image, multi-stage builds, and only copies necessary files to final image.
